items.title: Cloud credentials directory created without mode 0700 — API tokens world-listable on multi-user systems
items.intent: The cloud credentials directory must not be listable by other users — it stores API tokens and must be created with mode 0700 so directory contents are not exposed, matching the protection applied to credentials.ts file itself.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts line 53 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating the cloud/ directory. This creates the directory with default permissions (0755), making it world-readable. While credentials.json is written with mode 0600 (correct), any user on the system can ls the cloud/ directory to enumerate that cloud credentials exist. Same pattern: registry.ts line 57 and snapshot-registry.ts line 51 both call mkdirSync without mode. registry.ts is already covered by BUG-077. snapshot-registry.ts stores non-sensitive data (provider, region, version, timestamp) but should be consistent. Fix: pass { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 } to all three mkdirSync calls.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts lines 50-55 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in credentials.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Read src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts lines 48-53 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in snapshot-registry.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Run credentials tests and snapshot-registry tests — confirm both pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The cloud/ directory (deployDir/cloud/) is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
snapshot-registry parent directory is created with mode 0700
File permissions on credentials.json and snapshots.json remain unchanged at 0600

items.title: ops/integrations directory and .integration-manifest.json created without security modes — integration config world-listable
items.intent: ops/integrations and .integration-manifest.json must be created with restricted permissions — the manifest reveals which integrations (IMAP, SMTP, CalDAV) are configured on the agent, giving an attacker a precise capability map.
items.description: src/evolve/integrate/manifest.ts saveIntegrationManifest() (lines 35-43): line 38 calls mkdirSync(join(deployDir, MANIFEST_DIR), { recursive: true }) without mode — creates ops/integrations/ with default 0755. Line 39 calls writeFileSync(path, ..., 'utf-8') without mode option — creates .integration-manifest.json with default 0644. Both are wrong. The manifest stores integration state (name, enabled, version) and reveals exactly which integrations (email, calendar, tasks) are wired into the agent. No import from config/defaults.js exists today — needs DIR_MODE_SECRET and FILE_MODE_SECRET added.
acceptanceCriteria: On a multi-user system, a user other than the agent owner cannot list files in ops/integrations/ to discover which integrations (IMAP, SMTP, CalDAV) are configured
.integration-manifest.json is not readable by other users — integration state (name, enabled, version) remains private to the agent owner

items.title: workspace/skills directory and .skill-manifest.json created without security modes — skill capability enumeration
items.intent: workspace/skills/ and .skill-manifest.json must be created with mode 0700/0600 — the directory and manifest reveal which skills (and their vetting status) are installed, enabling targeted capability-specific attacks.
items.description: src/evolve/skills/lifecycle.ts saveManifest() (lines 49-56): line 54 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without mode — creates workspace/skills/ with default 0755. Line 55 calls await writeFile(manifestPath, ...) without mode option — creates .skill-manifest.json world-readable. The skill manifest stores skill name, source, install timestamp, vet result, and activation status — a capability map for the agent. FILE_MODE_EXEC is already imported from config/defaults.js; DIR_MODE_SECRET and FILE_MODE_SECRET are not.
acceptanceCriteria: On a multi-user system, a user other than the agent owner cannot list workspace/skills/ to enumerate which skills are installed
.skill-manifest.json is not readable by other users — skill name, source, vet result, and activation status remain private to the agent owner

items.title: workspace/tools directory and .tool-manifest.json created without security modes — tool capability enumeration
items.intent: workspace/tools/ and .tool-manifest.json must be created with mode 0700/0600 — listing the directory or reading the manifest reveals which agent tools (email, calendar, quote) are installed, informing targeted attacks against tool-specific integrations.
items.description: Two files both create workspace/tools/ without a mode: src/evolve/tools/lifecycle.ts line 77 (mkdirSync in installTool) and src/evolve/tools/manifest.ts line 43 (mkdirSync in saveToolManifest). manifest.ts line 44 also calls await writeFile without mode option — creates .tool-manifest.json world-readable. lifecycle.ts already imports FILE_MODE_EXEC from config/defaults.js. manifest.ts has no import from config/defaults.js at all.
acceptanceCriteria: On a multi-user system, a user other than the agent owner cannot list workspace/tools/ to enumerate which agent tools (email, calendar, quote) are installed
.tool-manifest.json is not readable by other users — installed tool details remain private to the agent owner

items.title: ops/rollback/skills snapshot directories created without mode 0700 — skill snapshots world-listable
items.intent: ops/rollback/skills/ and per-snapshot directories must be created with mode 0700 — they contain copies of skill scripts that were active before an install; enumerating them reveals skill history and previous capability state.
items.description: src/evolve/skills/rollback.ts has two mkdirSync calls without mode: line 51 in saveSnapshots() creates ops/rollback/skills/ with default 0755; line 71 in createSnapshot() creates the per-snapshot directory (e.g. ops/rollback/skills/snap-1234abcd) with default 0755. The per-snapshot directory gets cp'd with the entire workspace/skills/ tree. No import from config/defaults.js exists today.
acceptanceCriteria: On a multi-user system, a user other than the agent owner cannot list ops/rollback/skills/ to discover skill installation history
Per-snapshot directories (ops/rollback/skills/snap-*) are not listable by other users — previous skill state and rollback contents remain private

items.title: ops/audit directory re-created by trace.ts without mode 0700 — decision trace log world-readable
items.intent: trace.ts must create ops/audit/ with mode 0700 when it does not exist — scaffold.ts was fixed by BUG-095, but trace.ts independently creates this directory and would produce it world-readable if called before scaffold, or if the directory was removed.
items.description: src/evolve/memory/trace.ts logDecision() line 37 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without mode. TRACE_DIR='ops/audit' so this creates ops/audit/ with default 0755. BUG-095 fixed scaffold.ts to create ops/audit with 0700 at install time, but trace.ts is an independent call path — it creates the directory if it doesn't exist, and does so without the secure mode. No import from config/defaults.js exists in trace.ts today.
acceptanceCriteria: On a multi-user system, a user other than the agent owner cannot list ops/audit/ when the directory is first created by trace.ts rather than scaffold
ops/audit/ has mode 0700 regardless of which code path creates it — decision traces are never world-listable even if scaffold has not yet run
